title: Cenchrus lanatus
taxonomy: Cenchrus lanatus is classified under the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. It belongs to the tribe Paniceae and the genus Cenchrus. This classification is based on its anatomical and morphological characteristics. The species Cenchrus lanatus is also known as buffalo grass due to its ability to be used as fodder for livestock. It is widely distributed in various parts of the world, particularly in tropical and subtropical regions.
uses: Cenchrus lanatus, commonly known as the buffelgrass or black buffalo grass, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. Its roots, leaves, and stems are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is also believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating wounds and reducing swelling. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal uses of Cenchrus lanatus.

constituents: Cenchrus lanatus, also known as buffel grass, is a plant species with several reported active constituents. These constituents include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids. side_effects: Cenchrus lanatus, also known as buffalo grass or black buffalo grass, is a type of grass native to Africa. It is often used for forage and fodder. intro: Cenchrus lanatus, also known as the tall porcupine grass or black beet grass, is a perennial grass native to Africa and Asia. Traditionally, Cenchrus lanatus has been used in folk medicine for its numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, treating fever, and alleviating digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cenchrus lanatus include decoctions, infusions, and poultices, which are used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
